mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in supply capacity that reduces production costs and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate signals an improvement in labor market conditions that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in recovery periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in housing demand that increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals an expansion in supply capacity that reduces production costs and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in recovery periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment signals an improvement in labor market conditions that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index signals a decrease in purchasing power that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer spending that increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
